report_name: FBI and Google Disrupt Massive Chinese Phishing-as-a-Service Operation 'Outsider Enterprise'

threat_actors.name: Outsider Enterprise
threat_actors.description: A China-based cybercrime group operating a Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform. The group provided subscribers with phishing kits, hosting, and AI-powered content generation to carry out attacks, resulting in an estimated $1.9 billion in losses.
threat_actors.goals: To provide phishing tools and infrastructure to other criminals for financial gain.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The Outsider Enterprise operation relied heavily on SMS-based phishing (smishing) with links to fraudulent websites. Lures commonly involved fake notifications about missed packages or unpaid tolls.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Social Engineering LLM
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor leveraged Google's Gemini AI to automate and scale the creation of fraudulent website content and SMS lures, making their phishing pages more convincing and harder to detect.

attack_patterns.name: Masquerading
attack_patterns.description: The PhaaS operation relied on brand impersonation to trick victims into visiting malicious sites, using lures about missed packages or unpaid tolls.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The PhaaS platform used standard application layer protocols for its command-and-control infrastructure, which was targeted by law enforcement during the takedown.

tools.name: Google Gemini
tools.description: Abused by the Outsider Enterprise group to automate and scale the creation of convincing phishing sites and SMS lures.

campaigns.name: Operation Ghost Hook
campaigns.description: A coordinated law enforcement takedown operation led by the FBI, Google, and Lumen's Black Lotus Labs to disrupt the 'Outsider Enterprise' Phishing-as-a-Service network.

identities.name: FBI
identities.description: Collaborated in 'Operation Ghost Hook' to disrupt the Outsider Enterprise PhaaS network.

identities.name: Google
identities.description: Collaborated with the FBI in 'Operation Ghost Hook' and had its Gemini AI model abused by the threat actor.

identities.name: Lumen Black Lotus Labs
identities.description: Collaborated with the FBI and Google in the 'Operation Ghost Hook' takedown.

identities.name: Shopify
identities.description: A Shopify storefront was used by Outsider Enterprise for processing payments for their PhaaS subscriptions and was seized during the takedown.

infrastructure.name: Outsider Enterprise Phishing Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A vast network of phishing domains used to host fraudulent websites generated by the PhaaS platform.

infrastructure.name: Outsider Enterprise Admin Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Core administration domains used to manage the Phishing-as-a-Service operation, which were seized by the FBI.

infrastructure.name: Outsider Enterprise Payment Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A Shopify storefront and operator payment wallets used to process subscription payments for the PhaaS platform.

indicators.name: Outsider Enterprise Phishing Lure Keywords
indicators.pattern:
title: Outsider Enterprise Phishing Lure Keywords
id: f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4a7b-8c9d-e0f1a2b3c4d5
status: experimental
description: Detects URL patterns containing keywords commonly used in SMS phishing (smishing) lures by the Outsider Enterprise PhaaS operation.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/fbi-disrupts-massive-ai-powered-phishing-service-using-a-million-urls/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-14
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate shipping or toll road notifications that use these terms in URLs.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - 'missed-package'
      - 'unpaid-toll'
      - 'missed_package'
      - 'unpaid_toll'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects keywords such as 'missed package' and 'unpaid toll' used in the phishing lures of the Outsider Enterprise operation.

indicators.name: Potential Abuse of Google Gemini API for Phishing Content Generation
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Abuse of Google Gemini API for Phishing Content Generation
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-e0f1a2b3c4d5
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects network connections to the Google Gemini API endpoint from non-browser or non-standard processes.
  The 'Outsider Enterprise' PhaaS operation was observed abusing this API to generate phishing content.
  This is a behavioral rule and may require significant tuning to reduce false positives from legitimate applications using the Gemini API.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/fbi-disrupts-massive-ai-powered-phishing-service-using-a-million-urls/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-14
tags:
  - attack.aml.t0052.000
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate third-party applications, scripts, or developer tools that use the Gemini API.
level: low
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationHostname: 'generativelanguage.googleapis.com'
    Initiated: 'true'
  filter_legit_procs:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\python.exe'
      - '\node.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_procs
indicators.description: Detects potentially anomalous network traffic to the Google Gemini API, which was abused by Outsider Enterprise to generate phishing content.

relationships.source_name: Potential Abuse of Google Gemini API for Phishing Content Generation
Potential Abuse of Google Gemini API for Phishing Content Generation
